title: Epidemic Contact Graph
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Bioinformatics
description: Interactive SIR epidemic simulation with contact tracing visualization as a force-directed network graph

educational.topic: Epidemic Contact Graphs and Contact Tracing
learningObjectives: Understand the SIR compartmental model of disease spread
Visualize how contact networks enable or limit epidemic transmission
Explore the relationship between infection probability, contact rate, and outbreak size
Identify super-spreader patterns in contact tracing graphs
Interpret the basic reproduction number R0 and its dependence on model parameters

keyQuestions: How does increasing contacts per day affect the speed and size of the outbreak?
What combination of parameters causes the epidemic to die out before infecting everyone?
Can you identify the super-spreader nodes in the contact graph?
How does the recovery time affect the peak number of simultaneous infections?
commonMisconceptions: A high infection probability alone guarantees a large outbreak — contact rate matters equally
Recovered individuals can be reinfected — in the SIR model they are permanently immune
All infected people spread the disease equally — random contacts create uneven spread patterns
assessmentOpportunities: Students predict outbreak size given specific parameter combinations, then verify with simulation
Students identify which parameter changes would represent public health interventions (masking, social distancing, vaccination)
Students sketch the expected SIR curve shape before running the simulation
